Details: Bench- and pilot-scale studies suggest that removal of lithium from water using cation exchange media can be effective. Two bench-scale studies found maximum removals of 98 percent [2734] and 87.5 percent [2738]. Lithium removal in a pilot study [2735] was greater than 86 percent initially, declining with run time as the cation exchange media’s capacity was consumed. The authors concluded that the media’s exchange capacity could be completely restored by regeneration with sodium chloride.
